title: Transaction Management
content:
### @Transactional

`@Transactional` declaratively manages transactions. It can be applied to interfaces, classes, or methods.

### Propagation Levels

| Propagation | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `REQUIRED` (default) | Joins an existing transaction or creates a new one |
| `REQUIRES_NEW` | Suspends any existing transaction and creates a new one |
| `MANDATORY` | Requires an existing transaction; throws if none exists |
| `SUPPORTS` | Joins an existing transaction if one exists; runs non-transactionally otherwise |
| `NOT_SUPPORTED` | Suspends any existing transaction and runs non-transactionally |
| `NEVER` | Throws if a transaction exists |
| `NESTED` | Creates a savepoint within an existing transaction |

### Isolation Levels

| Level | Phenomena Prevented |
|---|---|
| `READ_UNCOMMITTED` | Nothing |
| `READ_COMMITTED` | Dirty reads |
| `REPEATABLE_READ` | Dirty, non-repeatable reads |
| `SERIALIZABLE` | Dirty, non-repeatable, phantom reads |

### Rollback Configuration

```java
@Transactional(rollbackFor = DataIntegrityViolationException.class,
               noRollbackFor = OptimisticLockException.class)
public void updateOrder(Order order) {
    // If DataIntegrityViolationException occurs, rollback
    // If OptimisticLockException occurs, commit anyway
}
```

### readOnly Optimization

Mark read operations with `readOnly = true` for performance hints.

```java
@Transactional(readOnly = true)
public Optional<User> findById(Long id) {
    return userRepository.findById(id);
}
```

quiz.question: What is the default propagation level in Spring transactions?
Which isolation level prevents dirty reads, non-repeatable reads, and phantom reads?
